summaryText: Pastor Travis Hearn opened the message by sharing the story of John Stephen Akhwari, the Tanzanian marathon runner who finished the 1968 Olympic race despite a severe injury, because his country sent him to finish, not just start. Pastor Travis used this powerful analogy to frame the Christian life as a marathon, emphasizing the biblical call to finish the race God has set before us. He noted that while many are great starters, fueled by initial excitement and infatuation, far fewer develop the perseverance to be finishers. The sermon provided three biblical keys to finishing strong. First, we must understand what race we are running—identifying God's specific purpose and vision for our lives, which shapes our preparation and mission, as described in Proverbs 29:18. Second, we must remove the distractions and hindrances that slow us down, whether sin, unhealthy relationships, or even good things that keep us from God's best, fixing our eyes on Jesus as instructed in Hebrews 12:1-2. Third, we must develop spiritual grit to keep pressing on, especially when the journey is hard. The most courageous step is often not the first, but the next one after a setback. Pastor Travis encouraged the congregation with Galatians 6:9, reminding them that a harvest awaits if they do not give up. He concluded with a passionate call to remember our value and purpose in Christ, to press on like the Apostle Paul did through immense hardship, and to build our lives on the firm foundation of Jesus.
mainPoints: Understand the specific race God has called you to run, as your vision defines your preparation and mission.
Remove every distraction and hindrance that slows you down, fixing your eyes solely on Jesus.
Develop spiritual grit to press on and take the next step, especially when it's hard, trusting that a harvest awaits those who do not give up.

versesMentioned.text: However, I consider my life worth nothing to me; my only aim is to finish the race and complete the task the Lord Jesus has given me—the task of testifying to the good news of God’s grace.
I have fought the good fight, I have finished the race, I have kept the faith.
Where there is no revelation, people cast off restraint; but blessed is the one who heeds wisdom’s instruction.
Do you not know that in a race all the runners run, but only one gets the prize? Run in such a way as to get the prize.
Everyone who competes in the games goes into strict training. They do it to get a crown that will not last, but we do it to get a crown that will last forever.
For I know the plans I have for you,” declares the Lord, “plans to prosper you and not to harm you, plans to give you hope and a future.

Therefore, since we are surrounded by such a great cloud of witnesses, let us throw off everything that hinders and the sin that so easily entangles. And let us run with perseverance the race marked out for us, fixing our eyes on Jesus, the pioneer and perfecter of faith.
You were running a good race. Who cut in on you to keep you from obeying the truth?
Keep your eyes straight ahead; ignore all sideshow distractions. Watch your step, and the road will stretch out smooth before you. Look neither right nor left; leave evil in the dust.
Let us not become weary in doing good, for at the proper time we will reap a harvest if we do not give up.

Not that I have already obtained all this, or have already arrived at my goal, but I press on to take hold of that for which Christ Jesus took hold of me. Brothers and sisters, I do not consider myself yet to have taken hold of it. But one thing I do: Forgetting what is behind and straining toward what is ahead, I press on toward the goal to win the prize for which God has called me heavenward in Christ Jesus.

Monday.scripture: Proverbs 29:18 (NIV) - Where there is no revelation, people cast off restraint; but blessed is the one who heeds wisdom’s instruction.
Monday.action: Write down one primary purpose God has placed on your heart. Ask Him to clarify the vision for this season of your life.
Monday.prayer: Lord, reveal the specific race You have for me. Help me see the finish line so I can build my life around Your purpose. Amen.

Tuesday.scripture: Hebrews 12:1 (NIV) - Therefore, since we are surrounded by such a great cloud of witnesses, let us throw off everything that hinders and the sin that so easily entangles.
Tuesday.action: Identify one distraction or 'good thing' that might be holding you back from God's best. Make a practical plan to limit its influence today.
Tuesday.prayer: God, give me courage to honestly name what is hindering me. Help me throw it off so I can run freely for You. Amen.

Wednesday.scripture: Galatians 6:9 (NIV) - Let us not become weary in doing good, for at the proper time we will reap a harvest if we do not give up.
Wednesday.action: Think of one area where you feel like giving up. Commit to taking one small, faithful 'next step' in that area today.
Wednesday.prayer: Father, when I feel weary, remind me of the harvest You have promised. Strengthen me to take the next step in faith. Amen.

Thursday.scripture: Philippians 3:13-14 (NIV) - Forgetting what is behind and straining toward what is ahead, I press on toward the goal to win the prize.
Thursday.action: Release one past failure or hurt to God in prayer. Choose to 'strain forward' by focusing on one positive action for your future.
Thursday.prayer: Jesus, I choose to forget what lies behind. Help me strain toward what is ahead and press on toward the prize of Your calling. Amen.

Friday.scripture: 1 Corinthians 9:25 (NIV) - Everyone who competes in the games goes into strict training. They do it to get a crown that will not last, but we do it to get a crown that will last forever.
Friday.action: Choose one spiritual discipline (e.g., prayer, Scripture reading, worship) and dedicate extra time to it today as 'training' for your race.
Friday.prayer: Holy Spirit, train me for the eternal race. Give me discipline and joy as I build spiritual endurance today. Amen.

Saturday.scripture: Acts 20:24 (NIV) - However, I consider my life worth nothing to me; my only aim is to finish the race and complete the task the Lord Jesus has given me.
Saturday.action: Reflect on your week. How did you move toward finishing your race? Encourage one other person in their journey to finish strong.
Saturday.prayer: God, my aim is to finish the task You gave me. Use my life to testify to Your grace, and help me encourage others to do the same. Amen.
